posts.title: Writing Mathematical Formulas in Markdown
posts.content:
# Writing Mathematical Formulas in Markdown

This blog now supports LaTeX math formulas! You can write beautiful mathematical expressions using inline and block syntax.

## Inline Math

Use single dollar signs for inline math: $E = mc^2$

Or use double backslashes with parentheses: \( \int_{a}^{b} f(x) dx \)

## Block Math

Use double dollar signs for block equations:

$$\frac{d}{dx}\left( \int_{a}^{x} f(t) dt \right) = f(x)$$

Or use double backslashes with square brackets:

\[\sum_{i=1}^{n} x_i = x_1 + x_2 + \cdots + x_n\]

## Examples

### Quadratic Formula
$$x = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$$

### Gaussian Distribution
$$f(x) = \frac{1}{\sigma\sqrt{2\pi}} e^{-\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{x-\mu}{\sigma}\right)^2}$$

### Matrix
$$\begin{bmatrix} a & b \\ c & d \end{bmatrix}$$

Enjoy writing mathematical content!
posts.excerpt: Learn how to write LaTeX math formulas in your blog posts with examples.

posts.title: Docker for Beginners
posts.content:
# Docker for Beginners

Docker has revolutionized how we deploy applications. This guide will help you understand the basics.

## What is Docker?

Docker is a platform for developing, shipping, and running applications in containers.

## Your First Dockerfile

```dockerfile
FROM node:18-alpine

WORKDIR /app

COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm install

COPY . .

EXPOSE 3000

CMD ["node", "server.js"]
```

## Building and Running

```bash
docker build -t my-app .
docker run -p 3000:3000 my-app
```

Containerization makes deployment much easier!
posts.excerpt: Learn Docker fundamentals and containerize your first application.
